users.securityQuestion: What is the name of my favorite spot on the couch?
What is the name of my inhaler brand?
What is the name of the space station I visited?
What is the name of my dog?
What is the name of my pet monkey?

flags.title: Flag of Nepal
flags.description: The only national flag that isn't quadrilateral! It consists of two stacked triangles. The crimson red is the color of the rhododendron, Nepal's national flower. Fun fact: The mathematical construction of this flag is defined in the constitution!

flags.title: Flag of Switzerland
flags.description: One of only two square sovereign-state flags (the other being Vatican City). The white cross doesn't extend to the edges, which is actually quite rare in flag design. Bazinga!

flags.title: Flag of Japan
flags.description: The Nisshoki or Hinomaru, meaning 'sun-mark flag'. A simple but powerful design - a red disc on white background representing the sun. Its simplicity makes it one of the most recognizable flags in the world.

flags.title: Flag of the European Union
flags.description: The twelve golden stars in a circle represent unity, solidarity, and harmony among the peoples of Europe. Fun fact: the number 12 was chosen for its symbolism of perfection and completeness, not for the number of member states!

flags.title: Flag of the Moon
flags.description: Technically there are 6 American flags on the Moon! They were all placed by Apollo missions. Due to solar radiation, they've probably turned white by now. Still counts!

flags.title: Flag of India
flags.description: The tricolor with the Ashoka Chakra in the center. The saffron represents courage, white represents peace and truth, and green represents fertility. The 24 spokes of the chakra represent the 24 hours of the day.

flags.title: Flag of Bhutan
flags.description: Features the Druk, the Thunder Dragon of Bhutanese mythology! The dragon holds jewels representing wealth. Orange represents Buddhist traditions while yellow represents the secular authority of the king.

flags.title: Flag of Canada
flags.description: The Maple Leaf! Did you know the 11-pointed maple leaf was chosen because it's the most distinctive when viewed from a distance or when the flag is waving? Pretty smart, eh?

flags.title: Flag of Poland
flags.description: Simple but elegant - white over red. The white represents peace and the red represents the blood shed for Poland's independence. It's been in use since 1831!

flags.title: Flag of Wales
flags.description: One of the few flags to feature a dragon! The red dragon (Y Ddraig Goch) has been associated with Wales for centuries. The green and white stripes represent the Tudor dynasty.

flags.title: Flag of Maryland
flags.description: Often considered the most distinctive U.S. state flag! It features the heraldic banner of George Calvert, 1st Baron Baltimore. The black and gold come from the Calvert family arms, while the red and white cross is from the Crossland family.

flags.title: Flag of South Korea
flags.description: The Taegukgi features the yin-yang symbol representing cosmic balance, surrounded by four trigrams from the I Ching representing heaven, earth, water, and fire. A perfect blend of philosophy and design!

flags.title: Flag of Mozambique
flags.description: The only national flag in the world featuring a modern weapon - an AK-47 rifle! It represents defense and vigilance. The rifle rests on an open book (education) and a hoe (agriculture). Truly unique!

flags.title: Flag of Brazil
flags.description: The celestial globe shows the sky over Rio de Janeiro on November 15, 1889 - the day Brazil became a republic! Each of the 27 stars represents a Brazilian state, and they're positioned astronomically correctly.

flags.title: Flag of Cyprus
flags.description: One of only two national flags featuring a map of the country itself (the other is Kosovo)! The copper-orange color represents the copper deposits that gave Cyprus its name. The olive branches below represent peace.

flags.title: Flag of Kosovo
flags.description: Along with Cyprus, one of only two flags showing a map of the country! The six stars represent Kosovo's major ethnic groups. Adopted in 2008 when Kosovo declared independence.

flags.title: Flag of Vatican City
flags.description: Along with Switzerland, one of only two square national flags! Features the crossed keys of Saint Peter (gold and silver) and the papal tiara. The smallest internationally recognized country in the world!

flags.title: Flag of Seychelles
flags.description: Features a unique oblique band design with five colors radiating from the bottom left corner! Blue (sky/sea), yellow (sun), red (people/work), white (harmony), green (land). One of the most colorful flags!

flags.title: Flag of South Africa
flags.description: One of the most colorful flags with six colors! Adopted in 1994 for the post-apartheid era. The Y-shape represents the convergence of diverse elements within South African society. A symbol of unity!

flags.title: Flag of Jamaica
flags.description: The only national flag that doesn't contain red, white, or blue! The black represents hardships overcome, gold represents sunshine and natural resources, and green represents hope and agricultural resources.

flags.title: Flag of Kiribati
flags.description: Features a frigatebird flying over a rising sun and ocean waves! The 17 rays of the sun represent the 16 Gilbert Islands plus Banaba. The three wave lines represent the three island groups. A beautiful Pacific design!

flags.title: Flag of Antigua and Barbuda
flags.description: The rising sun symbolizes the dawn of a new era! The V-shape represents victory, and the colors represent African heritage (black), hope (blue), and the beaches (white). Red represents the dynamism of the people.

flags.title: Flag of Sri Lanka
flags.description: Features a lion holding a sword, representing the Sinhalese people! The four bo leaves in the corners represent Buddhism. The stripes represent Tamil (orange) and Muslim (green) minorities. A flag of unity!

flags.title: Flag of Kazakhstan
flags.description: Features a golden steppe eagle beneath a 32-ray sun on a sky blue background! The ornamental pattern on the left is called 'koshkar-muiz' (horns of the ram). One of the most elegant flag designs!

flags.title: Flag of Papua New Guinea
flags.description: Divided diagonally with a bird-of-paradise on red and the Southern Cross on black! The bird-of-paradise is the national bird and represents the emergence into nationhood. Designed by a 15-year-old student!

flags.title: Flag of Albania
flags.description: Features a striking black double-headed eagle on red! The eagle is the symbol of freedom, dating back to the 15th century hero Skanderbeg. One of the oldest continuously used national symbols in Europe!

flags.title: Flag of Greenland
flags.description: The only Nordic country flag without a cross! The white and red represent ice and sun, with the circle representing the sun setting on the horizon over an iceberg. Adopted in 1985, designed by a local artist.

flags.title: Flag of Palau
flags.description: Features a golden full moon on a sky blue background! Unlike Japan's sun, this represents the moon, which is considered the optimal time for fishing, planting, and harvesting. Simple but deeply meaningful!

flags.title: Flag of Tonga
flags.description: Features a red cross on a white canton in the upper left corner! The red cross represents Christianity, which is central to Tongan culture. One of the few flags with a cross symbol!

flags.title: Flag of Turkmenistan
flags.description: One of the most detailed flags in the world! Features five carpet gul (designs) representing the five major tribes. The olive branches were added in 1997 for neutrality.

flags.title: Flag of Brunei
flags.description: Features the state emblem including a crescent (Islam), parasol (royalty), and wings (justice). The yellow represents the Sultan, and the stripes represent the chief ministers.

flags.title: Flag of Eswatini
flags.description: Features a traditional Nguni shield and two spears! The blue represents peace, yellow represents minerals, and red represents past battles. Formerly known as Swaziland!

flags.title: Flag of Maldives
flags.description: Green rectangle with a white crescent on a red field. The red represents the blood of heroes, green represents peace and prosperity, and the crescent represents Islam.

flags.title: Flag of Fiji
flags.description: Features the Union Jack and a shield with local symbols including a lion holding a cocoa pod, sugar cane, coconut palm, and bananas. A blend of British heritage and Pacific identity!

flags.title: Flag of Mauritius
flags.description: Four horizontal stripes: red (freedom), blue (ocean), yellow (independence), green (agriculture). Simple yet meaningful - representing the nation's aspirations!

flags.title: Flag of Cape Verde
flags.description: Ten stars representing the main islands arranged in a circle on blue with stripes of white and red. The blue represents the ocean and sky surrounding the islands!

flags.title: Flag of Comoros
flags.description: Features a green triangle with crescent and four stars representing the four main islands. One of the few flags with a crescent opening to the right!

flags.title: Flag of Samoa
flags.description: Features the Southern Cross constellation on red with a blue canton. Red represents courage, blue represents freedom, and white represents purity!

flags.title: Flag of Vanuatu
flags.description: Features a yellow Y-shape representing the island chain, with a boar's tusk emblem. The tusk is a symbol of prosperity worn by chiefs!

flags.title: Flag of Solomon Islands
flags.description: A blue and green flag divided diagonally by a yellow stripe. Five white stars represent the five main island groups!

flags.title: Flag of Tuvalu
flags.description: Light blue with the Union Jack and nine yellow stars representing the nine islands. The stars are arranged to mirror the actual geographical layout!

flags.title: Flag of Marshall Islands
flags.description: Features a diagonal band of orange and white on blue, with a large white star. The 24 points represent the electoral districts, and the four longer rays represent major centers!

comments.content: I never knew the construction was in the constitution! That's fascinating.
The triangles represent the Himalayan mountains. Pretty clever design!
Swiss design is always so clean and precise. Just like their watches!
The 12 stars have nothing to do with the number of member states. It's based on the symbolism of 12 representing completeness.
I always thought the stars were for the countries! Learn something new every day.
The Ashoka Chakra is actually based on the Lion Capital of Ashoka, which dates back to 250 BCE!
I love how scientifically they approached the design. Very methodical!
Dragons are so cool! Much better than boring eagles or lions.
Wow, that's a busy flag! Kind of gives me a headache looking at it.
Penny, it's heraldically complex, not 'busy'. There's a significant difference.
A gun on a flag? That's... intense.
It's actually an AK-47! Pretty bold design choice for a national symbol.
The astronomical accuracy is amazing! Each star position is scientifically correct.
Square flags are statistically rare. Only 0.01% of national flags are square!
I love how the colors radiate outward. Very artistic and modern design!
The Y-shape is such a powerful symbol of unity. Great design for post-apartheid South Africa.
No red, white, or blue? That's actually really unique! Most flags have at least one.
Thunder Dragon sounds like something from a video game. But it's real mythology!
The frigatebird on Kiribati's flag is known for its wingspan - up to 2.3 meters!
The Sinhalese lion has been a symbol since ancient times. Such rich history!
That steppe eagle is majestic! Kazakhstan really knew what they were doing with this design.
A 15-year-old designed this?! That's incredible. Some serious talent there.
Double-headed eagles are common in heraldry but Albania's is particularly striking.
Breaking the Nordic cross tradition was a bold move for Greenland!
The moon represents the optimal time for all activities in Palauan culture. Fascinating!
Monaco and Indonesia have almost identical flags! Only the proportions differ.
Tonga's flag has remained unchanged since 1875. That's constitutional stability!
Those carpet patterns (guls) are like QR codes - each tribe has a unique design!
The traditional shield design makes this one of the most culturally authentic flags.
Boar's tusks as symbols of wealth! Different cultures have such interesting value systems.
The Hinomaru is the epitome of minimalist design. So elegant!
I always thought this was just a simple red dot, but the meaning is really beautiful.
I wonder if any of those flags are still standing or if they've all fallen over by now.
Technically, they've turned white from solar radiation. So the Moon now has six surrender flags!
Sometimes the simplest designs are the most powerful. This flag proves it.
My grandmother used to tell me stories about Poland. This flag means a lot to our family.
The philosophy behind this design is fascinating. It's like the entire universe on a flag!
The trigrams represent fundamental concepts. Pretty advanced for flag design!
I love that they put the actual shape of their country on the flag. Very practical!
The copper color is a nice touch. Most flags don't reference their mineral resources!
Kosovo's flag is one of the newest in the world. Adopted in 2008!
Six stars for six ethnic groups. Very inclusive design approach.
The V for victory is such a powerful symbol. Great choice for a young nation!
The rising sun motif appears in several Caribbean flags. Interesting regional pattern!
That's a lot of symbolism packed into one flag. The parasol for royalty is unique!
The crescent and wings together create such an elegant design.
The green rectangle on red is really striking. Simple but memorable!
The Maldives changed their flag in 1965. Before that, it had a diagonal stripe!
The coat of arms has so much detail! You could study it for hours.
Four colors, four meanings. Clean and logical design approach!
The dodo bird used to be on this flag! They removed it in 1992.
Ten stars in a circle is such an elegant way to represent the islands!
The off-center band design is unusual but works really well here.
I always get confused about which way crescents face on flags. This one is unusual!
Each island gets its own star. Nice way to include everyone!
Another Southern Cross flag! New Zealand and Australia have them too.
The five stars represent the five main stars of the constellation. Astronomically accurate!
The diagonal stripe really makes the colors pop. Great composition!
Blue for the ocean, green for the land, yellow for the sunshine. Very Pacific!
The star arrangement matches the actual island positions. That's attention to detail!
Tuvalu is one of the smallest countries in the world but has a beautiful flag.
The diagonal rays are so dynamic! This flag looks like it's always in motion.
24 points for 24 electoral districts. Very democratic symbolism!
